{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Van Sales Representative",
  "description": "Applicant Recruiters Limited - Our client is&nbsp;a growing business specializing in FMCG. They are committed to providing top-notch products and exceptional customer service.

As part of their expansion, they are recruiting an energetic and motivated candidate to fill the position below:

Key Responsibilities

Job Position: Van Sales Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Ajao, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	Assist the sales team in identifying and reaching out to potential customers.
	Respond to customer inquiries via phone, whatsappp, and in-person.
	Identify and generate new sales opportunities through networking, cold calling, and relationship building.
	Conduct sales presentations and product/service demonstrations to prospective clients.
	Understand customer needs and offer solutions that align with their goals.
	Build and maintain long-term relationships with clients to ensure repeat business.
	Meet or exceed monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Provide feedback and insights on market trends and customer preferences to the sales manager.
	Maintain accurate records of all sales activities and customer interactions.
	Results-driven with a passion for achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Self-motivated, with strong organizational and time management skills.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of OND or its equivalent with 1-3 years of experience
	Must have a Valid Drivers License and LASDRI
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
	Proven experience as a Sales Representative or in a similar sales/customer service role.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays if required.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to build rapport and establish strong relationships with clients.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Opportunities for career growth and professional development.
	Supportive team environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;applicantrecruiters@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Sales Representative and Location&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

Van Sales Representative Job at Applicant Recruiters Limited




        
    

        
        Applicant Recruiters Limited - Our client is a growing business specializing in FMCG. They are committed to providing top-notch products and exceptional customer service.

As part of their expansion, they are recruiting an energetic and motivated candidate to fill the position below:

Key Responsibilities

Job Position: Van Sales Representative

Job Location: Ajao, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist the sales team in identifying and reaching out to potential customers.
	Respond to customer inquiries via phone, whatsappp, and in-person.
	Identify and generate new sales opportunities through networking, cold calling, and relationship building.
	Conduct sales presentations and product/service demonstrations to prospective clients.
	Understand customer needs and offer solutions that align with their goals.
	Build and maintain long-term relationships with clients to ensure repeat business.
	Meet or exceed monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Provide feedback and insights on market trends and customer preferences to the sales manager.
	Maintain accurate records of all sales activities and customer interactions.
	Results-driven with a passion for achieving and exceeding sales targets.
	Self-motivated, with strong organizational and time management skills.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a minimum of OND or its equivalent with 1-3 years of experience
	Must have a Valid Drivers License and LASDRI
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
	Proven experience as a Sales Representative or in a similar sales/customer service role.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays if required.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to build rapport and establish strong relationships with clients.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Opportunities for career growth and professional development.
	Supportive team environment.


Method of Application 
Intrested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using “Sales Representative and Location” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Social Media Manager",
  "description": "Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited is a dynamic and innovative fashion and garment production company headquartered in Isolo Lagos, Nigeria. Founded on a passion for excellence and a commitment to quality, we specialize in designing, producing, and delivering contemporary African?inspired apparel that celebrates culture, creativity, and individuality.&nbsp;At Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited, we blend traditional craftsmanship with modern fashion sensibilities to create garments that resonate with both local and international audiences. Our designs reflect the vibrant spirit of African heritage, tailored for today&rsquo;s trend?conscious consumers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement social media strategies aligned with brand goals
	Create, plan, and schedule engaging content (images, videos, reels, stories)
	Manage daily social media activities across platforms (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, X, etc.)
	Collaborate with photographers, designers, and content creators to produce high-quality visuals
	Monitor trends and leverage them to create relevant and engaging content
	Engage with followers, respond to comments and messages, and build a strong online community
	Analyze performance metrics and generate reports to optimize campaigns
	Run paid social media campaigns and promotions
	Ensure brand consistency in tone, voice, and visual identity
	Track competitor activities and industry trends


Key Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field
	1&ndash;4 years of experience managing social media accounts (preferably in fashion or lifestyle brands)
	Strong understanding of social media platforms and algorithms
	Experience with content creation and social media tools (e.g., Canva, Meta Business Suite, Hootsuite)
	Basic knowledge of social media analytics and performance tracking
	Excellent writing and communication skills.
	Content creation and storytelling
	Social media strategy and planning
	Analytics and data interpretation
	Creativity and trend awareness
	Community management
	Time management and organization.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;eleleifunanya821@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGER&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-29",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited"
    
  },

Social Media Manager Job at Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited




        
    

        
        Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited is a dynamic and innovative fashion and garment production company headquartered in Isolo Lagos, Nigeria. Founded on a passion for excellence and a commitment to quality, we specialize in designing, producing, and delivering contemporary African?inspired apparel that celebrates culture, creativity, and individuality. At Ojukwuu Afrostitches Limited, we blend traditional craftsmanship with modern fashion sensibilities to create garments that resonate with both local and international audiences. Our designs reflect the vibrant spirit of African heritage, tailored for today’s trend?conscious consumers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement social media strategies aligned with brand goals
	Create, plan, and schedule engaging content (images, videos, reels, stories)
	Manage daily social media activities across platforms (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, X, etc.)
	Collaborate with photographers, designers, and content creators to produce high-quality visuals
	Monitor trends and leverage them to create relevant and engaging content
	Engage with followers, respond to comments and messages, and build a strong online community
	Analyze performance metrics and generate reports to optimize campaigns
	Run paid social media campaigns and promotions
	Ensure brand consistency in tone, voice, and visual identity
	Track competitor activities and industry trends


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field
	1–4 years of experience managing social media accounts (preferably in fashion or lifestyle brands)
	Strong understanding of social media platforms and algorithms
	Experience with content creation and social media tools (e.g., Canva, Meta Business Suite, Hootsuite)
	Basic knowledge of social media analytics and performance tracking
	Excellent writing and communication skills.
	Content creation and storytelling
	Social media strategy and planning
	Analytics and data interpretation
	Creativity and trend awareness
	Community management
	Time management and organization.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using "SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGER" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Officer",
  "description": "RevoHealth (RVH) is a Nigerian Health Maintenance Organisation (HMO) established as a transformative health financing initiative committed to redefining access to healthcare across Africa. We are on a mission to drastically reduce out-of-pocket healthcare payments, eliminate financial barriers, and deploy technology to overcome physical access challenges, making healthcare truly accessible to underserved populations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose


	The Finance Officer is a critical mid-level role responsible for the financial integrity and regulatory alignment of the HMO. The position balances traditional corporate accounting with the specialized financial demands of the healthcare industry.


Core Strategic Pillars:


	Financial Operations &amp; Reporting: Managing the full accounting cycle, including monthly financial statements, budgetary oversight, and bank reconciliations to ensure organizational liquidity.
	Healthcare Value Chain Management: Direct oversight of Capitation and Fee-for-Service (FFS) payments. This involves close collaboration with medical audit teams to ensure claim authenticity and adherence to Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Revenue Assurance: Tracking and reconciling premium collections from corporate and individual enrollees to maintain a healthy cash flow.
	Regulatory &amp; Statutory Compliance: Ensuring strict adherence to NHIA financial guidelines and the timely remittance of statutory taxes (VAT, WHT, and PAYE).
	Data-Driven Analysis: Monitoring the Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) and identifying cost-saving opportunities within the clinical value chain to protect profit margins.


Candidate Profile


	Credentials: A degree in Accounting or Finance, ideally supported by professional certification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA).
	Experience: 3 &ndash; 5 years in a structured finance environment, with a strong preference for candidates from the insurance or healthcare sectors.
	Technical Skillset: Advanced proficiency in financial modelling (Excel) and enterprise accounting software (e.g., Sage or SAP).

Finance Officer Job at RevoHealth (RVH)




        
    

        
        RevoHealth (RVH) is a Nigerian Health Maintenance Organisation (HMO) established as a transformative health financing initiative committed to redefining access to healthcare across Africa. We are on a mission to drastically reduce out-of-pocket healthcare payments, eliminate financial barriers, and deploy technology to overcome physical access challenges, making healthcare truly accessible to underserved populations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance Officer is a critical mid-level role responsible for the financial integrity and regulatory alignment of the HMO. The position balances traditional corporate accounting with the specialized financial demands of the healthcare industry.


Core Strategic Pillars:


	Financial Operations & Reporting: Managing the full accounting cycle, including monthly financial statements, budgetary oversight, and bank reconciliations to ensure organizational liquidity.
	Healthcare Value Chain Management: Direct oversight of Capitation and Fee-for-Service (FFS) payments. This involves close collaboration with medical audit teams to ensure claim authenticity and adherence to Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Revenue Assurance: Tracking and reconciling premium collections from corporate and individual enrollees to maintain a healthy cash flow.
	Regulatory & Statutory Compliance: Ensuring strict adherence to NHIA financial guidelines and the timely remittance of statutory taxes (VAT, WHT, and PAYE).
	Data-Driven Analysis: Monitoring the Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) and identifying cost-saving opportunities within the clinical value chain to protect profit margins.


Candidate Profile


	Credentials: A degree in Accounting or Finance, ideally supported by professional certification (e.g., ICAN, ACCA).
	Experience: 3 – 5 years in a structured finance environment, with a strong preference for candidates from the insurance or healthcare sectors.
	Technical Skillset: Advanced proficiency in financial modelling (Excel) and enterprise accounting software (e.g., Sage or SAP).
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Invoicer (Female) Job at Macden Communications Limited




        
    

        
        Macden Communications Limited - The company is into the sales, supply and distribution of beverages and is a major distributor for Nigerian Breweries Limited, International Breweries PLC, Guiness Nigerian Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Invoicer (Female)
Job Location: Oshodi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Invoice Processing
	Accuracy Verification
	Recording of Invoices
	Discrepancy Resolution


Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The working time and days are from 8am to 6pm and from Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays
	Interested applicants should have working experience of between 1-3 years and must be knowledgeable in business and good with computer softwares
	Applicants must be resident within Oshodi, Mushin and any other close by surrounding areas.


Salary
N75,000 - N80,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more inquiries, candidates should call: 09063461304.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Mark Calthers Consulting is a Management Consulting firm with a broad spectrum of Human Resource Management and Business Consulting services. We work with our clients from visioning through strategy design to implementation and capacity building with a view to realizing and sustaining value.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Gbagada, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role


	Identify and close new B2B opportunities, manage corporate client relationships, and execute strategies aligned with revenue targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Drive client acquisition and revenue growth
	Prospect, qualify, and close deals
	Prepare proposals and presentations
	Manage long-term client relationships
	Conduct market research and track pipeline
	Represent MCC at meetings and networking events.


Requirements


	Degree in Economics, Business, Marketing, or related fields
	1 - 2 years of experience in Business Development/Sales
	Proven track record of meeting revenue targets
	Consulting/professional services experience is an advantage
	Strong negotiation, communication &amp; results-driven mindset.


What We Offer


	Competitive pay (Salary: N150,000 - N250,000 monthly)&nbsp;+ performance incentives
	Career growth opportunities
	Exposure to high-value corporate clients.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their latest CV and a brief cover letter &nbsp;to:&nbsp;jobs@markcalthers.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Business Development Executive Application&rdquo; as&nbsp;the subject of the mail.",

Business Development Executive Job at Mark Calthers Consulting




        
    

        
        Mark Calthers Consulting is a Management Consulting firm with a broad spectrum of Human Resource Management and Business Consulting services. We work with our clients from visioning through strategy design to implementation and capacity building with a view to realizing and sustaining value.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location: Gbagada, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role


	Identify and close new B2B opportunities, manage corporate client relationships, and execute strategies aligned with revenue targets.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Drive client acquisition and revenue growth
	Prospect, qualify, and close deals
	Prepare proposals and presentations
	Manage long-term client relationships
	Conduct market research and track pipeline
	Represent MCC at meetings and networking events.


Requirements


	Degree in Economics, Business, Marketing, or related fields
	1 - 2 years of experience in Business Development/Sales
	Proven track record of meeting revenue targets
	Consulting/professional services experience is an advantage
	Strong negotiation, communication & results-driven mindset.


What We Offer


	Competitive pay (Salary: N150,000 - N250,000 monthly) + performance incentives
	Career growth opportunities
	Exposure to high-value corporate clients.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their latest CV and a brief cover letter  to: [email protected] using “Business Development Executive Application” as the subject of the mail.
